_comment: Cross-language ranking parity fixture. A fixed real MovieLens (ml-latest-small) scenario: 10 candidate movies, 2 users, scored through the native RT-J backend. Every binding loads the SAME data + the SAME precomputed MiniLM embeddings (embeddings.tsv, normalize=False) so results are comparable. Guards against the two ranking bugs found on 2026-07-18: (a) Python emitting no candidate cells, (b) Java emitting no target token for cell-less entity tables — both produced the degenerate enumeration-order ranking.
query: PREDICT LIST_DISTINCT(ratings.movie_id) OVER (60 DAYS FOLLOWING RANK TOP 5) FROM users WHERE users.user_id IN :ids

title_of.260: Star Wars: Episode IV - A New Hope (1977)

invariants._comment: Cross-language checks that hold despite float-level tie ordering. degenerate_order is what BOTH bugs produced (candidate enumeration order); a correct ranking must NOT equal it. top1 (Silence of the Lambs) is the strongest, tie-free signal and is stable across bindings.

per_binding_golden._comment: Each binding's full captured top-5 (movie_id order). They agree on top1 and the top-5 SET but differ within sub-0.004 score ties at lower ranks — a property of the weak model signal on this task, not a bug. Used as a per-binding regression guard. java filled in once the target-token fix lands.
